title: CombineFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Combine features in a component and supports the ability to create new Combine features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a CombineFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the combine you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the CombineFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified combine feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified combine feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of combine features in the collection. This property returns nothing in the case where the feature is non-parametric.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: CombineFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created CombineFeature object or null if the creation failed. This function returns nothing in the case where the feature is non-parametric.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A CombineFeatureInput object that defines the desired combine. Use the createInput method to create a new CombineFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the CombineFeatureInput object) to define the combine.

related_samples.name: combineFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the combineFeatures.add method. To use this sample, have a design open that contains at least two bodies. When you run the sample, you will be prompted to select the bodies and they will joined.

classType.title: CombineFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: CombineFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a CombineFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the combine you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the CombineFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created CombineFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: targetBody
parameters.description: A BRep body that represents the blank body.

parameters.name: toolBodies
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection containing one or more BRep bodies that represent tool bodies.

related_samples.name: combineFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the combineFeatures.add method. To use this sample, have a design open that contains at least two bodies. When you run the sample, you will be prompted to select the bodies and they will joined.

item.title: CombineFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified combine feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified. This property returns nothing in the case where the feature is non-parametric.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: CombineFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified combine feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: CombineFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of combine features in the collection. This property returns nothing in the case where the feature is non-parametric.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = combineFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: CombineFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = combineFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: CombineFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = combineFeatures_var.objectType
